meta.site_subtitle: Evidence, thresholds, and four forecasts for advanced AI.
meta.site_description: A public record of how close frontier systems are to the AI 2027 capability ladder, how four forecasts differ, and which observable tests would change the picture.
meta.scoring_convention: Milestones score on publicly verifiable evidence only: system cards, third-party evaluations, and documented deployments.
meta.internal_lag_explanation: The internal lag is used only to translate public-verification dates into spine-comparable internal-existence dates. The current draft value is four months and is contestable.
meta.update_protocol: Edit data/canonical.json, run npm run validate, and commit. Forecast records are append-only; a revision adds a new record and links the prior record through superseded_by.
meta.progress_methodology: Each capability score is the weighted completion of observable criteria derived from that AI 2027 agent definition. Criterion weights sum to 100%; each criterion is scored from 0 to 100% using only public evidence. The site validates the arithmetic and links every criterion to its evidence.
meta.progress_disclaimer: Progress scores are rubric-completion estimates, not probabilities that a capability has arrived and not measurements on a single benchmark. The evidence is incomplete, private frontier systems may be ahead, and the rubric itself is contestable.
meta.distribution_warning: Distribution bands are marginal forecasts, not sampled paths. Do not read one percentile column as a coherent scenario unless correlation is modeled explicitly.
meta.sample_warning: SAMPLE marks structurally necessary content that was not supplied by the source corpus. It is not forecast evidence.

headline_stats.label: Public capability
headline_stats.detail: Public systems now match the carefully managed coding-and-research employee described by AI 2027; later agent levels remain incomplete.

headline_stats.label: AC → ASI median gap
headline_stats.detail: Daniel 1.8 y · Codex 2.8 y · Claude 5.0 y — the live disagreement is takeoff speed, not timelines.

headline_stats.label: Most diagnostic next test
headline_stats.detail: The registered METR 80% time-horizon threshold is the next dated tripwire.

headline_stats.label: Agent-2 rubric
headline_stats.detail: Up five points after new public evidence on persistent cyber agency and AI-assisted mathematical discovery; continual learning and measured R&D uplift still bind.

briefing.title: Capability is advancing faster than the control evidence.
briefing.description: Public systems satisfy the Agent-1 bundle and show material pieces of Agent-2. The strongest new evidence comes from persistent long-horizon behavior, independent cyber evaluations, and AI-assisted research results; reliable autonomy, continual learning, and measured AI-R&D acceleration remain unresolved.
briefing.pace_detail: AI Futures' July 2026 preliminary analysis estimates that quantitative progress is running at roughly three quarters of the pace depicted in the frozen AI 2027 scenario. This is scenario calibration, not a new forecast distribution.

pace_secondary.label: Independent tracker
pace_secondary.detail: The independent AI 2027 Tracker grades 27 of 53 predictions extracted from the scenario as confirmed, ahead, or on track as of July 2026, and observes that several risks are arriving before the raw capabilities that were supposed to produce them.

lenses.label: Capability
lenses.detail: Expert coding and managed research work are public; safe workday-scale autonomy and a measured 3x AI-R&D multiplier are not.

lenses.label: Control readiness
lenses.detail: Trajectory monitoring and frontier frameworks are developing, while recent evaluations exposed containment and monitor failures.

lenses.label: Open-weight diffusion
lenses.detail: The leading open-weight cyber model in AISI testing matched closed models released four to seven months earlier; openness and transparency remain separate questions.

tracks.name: AI 2027 original scenario
tracks.description: The frozen April 2025 scenario dates, kept as a fixed historical reference. Author updates never edit this spine; they enter the record as evidence items.

tracks.name: Codex capability forecast
tracks.description: A July 2026 provisional distribution with a 2032 WSI median. Neither an immediate AC-to-ASI jump nor a flat multi-year widening: a real AC-to-SAR delay while research taste binds, then renewed compression once systems select their own research agendas.

tracks.name: Claude widening thesis
tracks.description: The widening thesis, with a 2035 WSI median: automating perspiration multiplies experiments but does not manufacture research taste to select among them, so gaps stretch through SAR before compression resumes.

tracks.name: Daniel Kokotajlo Q1 2026
tracks.description: Daniel Kokotajlo's pinned April 2026 all-things-considered model output, moved toward shorter timelines by faster agentic-coding progress, a faster METR time-horizon doubling estimate, and a lower AC horizon requirement.

views.label: Overview
views.title: How close are we to the next AI capability level?
views.description: A compact read of the public evidence first, followed by four forecast distributions. Open any score, range, or signal when you want the underlying definitions, assumptions, and sources.

views.label: Forecasts
views.title: Four timelines, and the assumptions that separate them
views.description: Compare the full capability ladder, inspect one threshold at a time, and trace the bottlenecks that pull each forecast earlier or later. Outside views provide context without being treated as interchangeable definitions.

views.label: Tests
views.title: Dated tripwires and structural monitors
views.description: Dated tripwires carry locked thresholds and consequences. Structural monitors are important but not falsely presented as pre-registered falsifiers when the source material does not supply a quantitative deadline.

views.label: Evidence
views.title: Observations, weighed by how much they cut
views.description: Every entry is an authored summary of a public source, tagged by milestone, direction, and diagnosticity. Filters narrow the view; the underlying record only ever grows.

views.label: Drivers
views.title: What is pulling the timeline in each direction
views.description: The evidence does not move every bottleneck together. Some constraints are easing, some still bind, and some remain unresolved because public evaluations cannot yet test them cleanly.

views.label: Changelog
views.title: Every material change, newest first
views.description: Forecast revisions, evidence additions, and protocol amendments, kept as an append-only public record. Nothing is silently overwritten.

views.label: Methodology
views.title: How this record is graded
views.description: Milestones score on publicly verifiable evidence. Internal existence is inferred through a contestable four-month lag, forecast records are append-only, and every revision names the evidence that moved it.

views.label: Glossary
views.title: The terms this record depends on
views.description: Forecast disagreements are often definition disagreements in disguise. Every recurring term on this site is defined here once, so a date, a percentile, or a status can be read the same way on every page.

milestones.name: Agent 1
milestones.operational_definition: A highly capable coding and AI-research assistant that works quickly on well-specified problems but still needs careful management for long-horizon work.
milestones.spine_description: Knows practically every programming language, solves well-specified coding problems extremely quickly, and matches top expert humans on eight-hour well-defined AI research-engineering tasks, while remaining unreliable on simple long-horizon tasks.

milestones.name: Agent 2
milestones.operational_definition: Continual-learning research engineer; about 3x AI R&D uplift.
milestones.spine_description: Qualitatively almost as good as the top human experts at research engineering, as good as the 25th percentile OpenBrain scientist at research taste, and able to triple OpenBrain's algorithmic progress.

milestones.name: Automated Coder
milestones.operational_definition: An AGI company would sooner remove all human software engineers than stop using AI coding labor.
milestones.spine_description: A later AI Futures Model milestone that falls between Agent-2 and Agent-3 in the supplied mapping.

milestones.name: Superhuman Coder
milestones.operational_definition: About 900x effective elite coding labor: best-coder quality, 30x speed, and 30 copies.
milestones.spine_description: A fast and cheap superhuman coder. OpenBrain runs a workforce equivalent to 50,000 copies of the best human coder sped up by 30x.

milestones.name: Superhuman AI Researcher
milestones.operational_definition: Qualitatively better than any human at AI research.
milestones.spine_description: An individual copy, running at human speed, is already qualitatively better at AI research than any human.

milestones.name: Superintelligent AI Researcher
milestones.operational_definition: The research-capability gap is far beyond SAR: as far beyond SAR as SAR is beyond the median researcher.
milestones.spine_description: An AI system vastly better than the best human researcher at AI research.

milestones.name: Top-Expert-Dominating AI
milestones.operational_definition: At least as good as top human experts at virtually all cognitive tasks.
milestones.spine_description: A later AI Futures Model milestone; it does not have a distinct named agent in the frozen AI 2027 ladder.

milestones.name: Artificial Superintelligence
milestones.operational_definition: The gap to the best humans is twice the best-human-to-median-professional gap at virtually all cognitive tasks.
milestones.spine_description: An AI system much better than the best human at every cognitive task.

milestones.name: Wildly Superintelligent AI
milestones.operational_definition: The continuity endpoint of JJ's forecasting framework: wildly superintelligent AI, far past the ASI threshold. Not a standardized AI Futures milestone.
milestones.spine_description: A continuity term rather than a standardized AI Futures milestone.
